Document:
# Roleplay Rule 3 - Roleplay a believable character. SS14 is a roleplaying game, it is assumed that the character that you choose to play as fits within the universe and would be employable by Nanotrasen. Attempt to have your character behave in an appropriate way for their personality and occupation.
Examples - It’s unlikely an underpaid elderly janitor would jump at the first opportunity to get into a fist fight. - It's probably more likely for a bartender to prioritize the safety of their patrons than running off to face a threat themselves. Consider alternative options like sheltering them in the hidden back room or escorting them to a safer location instead. - A Chief Medical Officer is unlikely to have finished their education and risen through the career ranks by being unreliable, unhinged or otherwise unfit for duty.
You may play characters that don’t follow conventionally expected behavior, but when doing so try to keep things consistent.
When it’s a life or death situation, such as a station ending threat, a character may very justifiably break their usual behavior patterns.
## Use proper language when communicating in game:
- Do not use texting/messaging acronyms (ex: "lol", "wtf", "brb", "lmao", "thx", "sgtm") or emoticons (ex: ":)", "xD") in-character. - Do not mention out-of-character (OOC) concepts like game admins or developers in character. - Do not use emotes to bypass muted or accented speech. - Do not use extremely low effort or impossible emotes.
## Examples Things you should not do: - Say "lol did u c wat just happened" using in-character chat. - Say "an admin exploded him" using in-character chat. - Emote "can you give me some cheese" as a mouse. - Emote "motions for you to order guns" or "asks you to order guns in sign language" as a mime.
Things you could do instead: - Say "haha did you see what just happened?" - Say "god blew him up" or "centcomm must have bluespaced a bomb to him" - Point at cheese - Point at the cargo order console then emote "shoots finger guns"
